description_long: This object is a white plastic chair that has been repurposed as a makeshift storage unit, with two large cardboard boxes stacked on its seat and backrest. The boxes are variously labeled, with one showing the 'Gold Winner' brand and another marked with 'Go Mart', and both are partially open, revealing some contents inside. The chair itself is visibly worn, with some dirt and possible damage to the legs and seat. The cardboard boxes are taped and show signs of use, with some flaps open and others closed, and they are not perfectly aligned, giving the structure a haphazard appearance. The overall construction appears unstable and improvised, likely intended for temporary storage or transport of lightweight items. The combination of materials and the way the boxes are stacked suggest this object might be found in an urban alley, garage, or street-side setting.
description: A worn white plastic chair stacked with two large, taped cardboard boxes, forming a makeshift storage unit. The boxes are labeled and partially open, giving the object an improvised, urban look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open flaps of the top box and the stacked arrangement on the chair's seat.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the 'Gold Winner' label and the way the boxes overhang the chair's edge.
description_view_2: Opposite side view showing the alignment of the boxes and the visible tape on the lower box.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the chair's perforated backrest and the closed flaps of the lower box.
